metadata.title:
RULING TOUGH


ON HARTMAN

UNFAIR TO CUT HIM DOWN TO
HILL LINES REDUCTION
WHEN HE HAS TO HAUL so
FAR, HIS MAN SAYS.

pages.raw_text:
After a five weeks study and
conference with other traffic Off.
Gals of the Hartman lines. r. b.
Miller, general freight agent in the
northwestern territory for the Har-
riman interests, declares the Spo-
kane freight rate case to be sad
thing for Hartman Says Miller:


Il do not know that the carriers
interested have reached any def
mite conclusions with regard to the
Spokane rate case. but so far as
our lines are concerned We have
petitioned the interstate commerce
commission to so modify its order
as il will not apply via the o. r.
& N. and the Union Pacific compa-
MES.


This order establishes certain
class and commodity rates from
ST Paul and Chicago to Spokane
and as we view it, is directed
mainly at the northern lines. Spo-
kane is located on the main lines
of the Great Northern and North
ern Pacific and o. r. & n. from ST.
: Paul to Spokane is substantially %
per cent greater than via the d.
rect lines.


Consequently it would be mani
testify unfair to require the Union
Pacific and the o. r. & ~ compa-
MES to apply via their longer route
the rates which the, commission
have established as reasonable
rates for the much shorter service
via the Northern Pacific and Great
Northern"
